rag_rules: Fix type mismatches in AGREE expressions by ensuring left and right sides of binary operations are of the same type, particularly when referencing component ports or signals.
Resolve 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors by tracing unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations and correcting or removing the problematic clauses.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, ensure all expressions involving component ports or signals are type-consistent and reference only visible identifiers.
When validating AADL models, prioritize fixing root causes of type errors before adding new architecture elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE binary expressions must have operands of matching types; use visible AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex scope.
Component type contracts should reference valid port types and avoid using implementation-specific features unless properly scoped.
anti_patterns: Avoid adding new architecture elements to resolve AGREE type errors; instead, trace and fix the root cause of the type mismatch or unresolved identifier.
Do not place component type assumptions or guarantees in implementation scopes; they must reside in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about unreachable or inadequate port references
AGREE validator diagnostics about component type contracts in implementation scopes
